rulings.Case Title: WAYNE A. MCFADDEN VS. MARY M. CHIARENZA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 18, 2010, line 2. PLAINTIFF WAYNE MCFADDEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MSJ as to Weinberg: Grant, barred by statute of limitations. See UMF 11, 13, 16, 26. MSA as to Issues 6, 7, and 9 (whether Cross-Complainant can prove damages): Deny. Cross-defendants fail to meet their statutory burden under CCP 437c(p)(2) of showing that these causes of action have no merit. Burden did not shift to cross-complainant. MSA as to Issues 6, 7, and 9 on statute of limitations grounds as to McFadden: Deny. Trindade v. Superior Court, 29 Cal. App. 23d 857, 860 (1973). MSA as to Issues 1-5 (whether Cross-Complainant can prove damages): Deny. Cross-defendants fail to meet their statutory burden under CCP 437c(p)(2) of showing that these causes of action have no merit. Burden did not shift to cross-complainant. MSA as to Issue 10 (emotional distress damages): Deny. See CCP 437c(f)(1), Catalano v. Superior Court, 82 Cal. App. 4th 91, 96-97 (2000). MSA as to Issue 8: Moot, in light of Court's Order of November 10, 2010 (Judge Alvarado) granting judgment on the pleadings as to the 8th cause of action for fraud, with leave to amend. No second amended cross-complaint alleging fraud is before the Court at this time. MSJ as to McFadden: Deny, in light of rulings above. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FAYE BEYELER VS. SARAH BEYELER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenae
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 18, 2010, line 12. DEFENDANTS SARAH BEYELER, JONATHAN BEYELER, ROCHELLE BEYELER'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAE. Deny without prejudice to defendants seeking a protective order limiting who may see the information and for what purpose. Motion fails to attach required Separate Statement pursuant to CRC 3.1345(a)(5). On the merits, defendants have not established why records sought are covered by the right of privacy. Even assuming a right to privacy does exist for the information sought, plaintiff has overcome the burden by showing that information sought is directly relevant and essential to proving her claim. The request is narrowly tailored. The public interest in obtaining the discovery outweighs any claimed right to privacy. Pursuant to CCP 1987.2, grant request for award of plaintiff's reasonable expenses incurred in opposing the motion, which was made without substantial justification, in the amount of $2600 (8 hours x $325 hourly attorney fee charged by plaintiff's counsel). Grant request to impose this amount jointly and severally between and among defendants and their counsel. =(302/MJM)
